digest.component_or_area: review-bot review queue and backfill reconciler
propositions: On autodeploy restart the review-bot cancels its worker without draining the in-memory review queue, so acknowledged-but-unreviewed webhooks are silently dropped.
Recovery of dropped events falls entirely to the slow backfill reconciler (300s interval, sequential full reviews), causing changes to stall for tens of minutes.
The lifespan shutdown should await queue.join() bounded by SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_SECONDS before cancelling the worker so queued events drain, with undrained items left to the reconciler.
Docker's default 10s stop_grace_period SIGKILLs the service mid-drain, so review-bot needs a longer stop_grace_period (60s).
reconcile_once calls review_and_vote with no timeout, so a single hung backfill review freezes all backfill indefinitely.
The reconciler should wrap each review in a per-review timeout matching the live worker, emit a marker on timeout, and continue to the next candidate.
